word_list.word: he that hath a full purse never wanted a friend
word_list.pronunciation: hiː ðæt hæθ ɐ fˈʊl pˈɜːs nˈɛvɚ wˈɑːntᵻd ɐ fɹˈɛnd

senses.definition: used to suggest that people who have wealth or resources are often able to attract and keep friends more easily

word_list.word: a great dowry is a bed full of brambles
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡɹˈeɪt dˈaʊɹi ɪz ɐ bˈɛd fˈʊl ʌv bɹˈæmbəlz

senses.definition: used to suggest that having a large dowry or focusing solely on material wealth in a marriage can lead to difficulties, challenges, or even unhappiness

examples.x: John's parents urged him to marry a wealthy woman with a large dowry, but he knew that a great dowry is a bed full of brambles, and he wanted to marry for love, not money.

word_list.word: no man loves his fetters, be they made of gold
word_list.pronunciation: nˈoʊ mˈæn lˈʌvz hɪz fˈɛɾɚz biː ðeɪ mˌeɪd ʌv ɡˈoʊld

senses.definition: used to suggest that the pursuit of material wealth should not come at the expense of personal freedom or genuine happiness

word_list.word: you cannot take it with you when you (die|go)
word_list.pronunciation: juː kænˈɑːt tˈeɪk ɪt wɪð juː wɛn juː dˈaɪ ɡˈoʊ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that material possessions are ultimately temporary and that focusing on relationships, experiences, and personal growth are more meaningful in the long run

word_list.word: the best things in life are free
word_list.pronunciation: ðə bˈɛst θˈɪŋz ɪn lˈaɪf ɑːɹ fɹˈiː

senses.definition: used to imply that the most valuable things in life, such as happiness and love, cannot be bought, emphasizing the importance of prioritizing experiences and relationships over wealth

examples.x: As we sat on the beach watching the sunset, Steve turned to me and said "The best things in life are free, aren't they?"

word_list.word: man (does not|cannot) live by bread alone
word_list.pronunciation: mˈæn dʌznˌɑːt kænˈɑːt lˈaɪv baɪ bɹˈɛd ɐlˈoʊn

senses.definition: used to emphasize that in addition to basic necessities like food and shelter, people have emotional, social, and spiritual needs that must be fulfilled to live a happy life

examples.x: John realized that while money was important, he needed more than just financial security to be happy because man does not live by bread alone.

word_list.word: a moneyless man goes fast through the market
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ mˈʌnɪləs mˈæn ɡoʊz fˈæst θɹuː ðə mˈɑːɹkɪt

senses.definition: used to imply that without money to spend, one is not interested in the market, and so will not spend much time there

examples.x: When Sarah was a college student with limited funds, she found herself less interested in shopping and spending time in commercial areas, demonstrating how a moneyless man goes fast through the market.

senses.definition: used to imply that having a lot of money or material possessions does not necessarily lead to happiness or contentment in life

word_list.word: the things you own end up owning you
word_list.pronunciation: ðə θˈɪŋz juː ˈoʊn ˈɛnd ˌʌp ˈoʊnɪŋ juː

senses.definition: used to imply that excessive attachment to material possessions can lead to a loss of personal freedom and control

examples.x: The homeowner who had filled his house with expensive furniture and artwork found himself constantly worried about the safety and maintenance of his possessions, demonstrating just how the things you own end up owning you.

senses.definition: used to imply that personal happiness, health, relationships, and personal growth are more important than money, encouraging people to prioritize these values over financial success

examples.x: The artist chose to pursue his passion, even though it didn't pay as well as other careers, because he believed that money is not everything.
